name: Stop The Vandal
Description:
Someone has been graffitiing the Town's walls with red slime, scrawling insulting statements like 'Humans Suck!', as well as threats to the Town as a whole. The cleaning costs and paperwork involved have been astronomical. Please find the source of this vandalism and stop it before I have an aneurysm. Payment is 1000 Eros. 
- Elena

theScene: Having now been walking through the Forest for quite some time and yet having seen neither hair nor hide of the vandal you were sent to apprehend, you begin to wonder if you have somehow been on the wrong track all along... until you fortuitously come upon a trail of distinctive cherry-red slime on the ground! Following it, you eventually come upon a clearing where you can hear a feminine voice loudly shouting.

Freezing in alert and wondering if you've somehow been spotted already, you look around in a panic, expecting to see at any moment a new opponent bear down on you... instead of which you hear an embarrassed little cough from the glade ahead.
{size=-5}{i}Hrm...{/i} No, no... Uuh... {i}Grovel{/i}? Or maybe... {i}Worship me{/i}? Ugh, being an evil mastermind is hard...{/size}
Still feeling rather confused, but sensing no immediate threat as the mumbling soliloquy continues, you decide to enter the clearing and confront this 'Perpetua'.

As you suspected, 'Perpetua' turns out to be just the red slime you were looking for, the diminutive yet seductively curvy goo girl sloshing to and fro in deep thought as she tests one quip after another, quite obviously so preoccupied with her rehearsal that she has failed to notice you, the monster vandal absentmindedly running a hand through her tendril-like mop of 'hair' as she mutters.

{size=-3}{i}This is the end for you{/i}, maybe? Hm, sounds a bit too mean... {i}Say your prayers{/i}? ... {i}Pray to Venereae, cur{/i}... Now that's just silly... Hmm... What would the Black Knight say? Something super cool like-{/size}
Jumping in surprise at your unheard approach, the red slime whirls around with a sputtering squeak of alarm.
{b}Ah!?{/b} A hu-human?! What are you doing here?!

Her fists peevishly clenched and her face set in an indignant pout at your incidental eavesdropping, she stares at you angrily in a way that quite pointedly demands an explanation, prompting you to take out your Grimoire and point to the Quest, which Perpetua curiously peers at as you explain.
I'm here to stop you from vandalizing the Town.
Immediately, her expression somehow switches to a beaming smile as she joyfully claps her hands with a gooey splat, before proudly crossing her arms and thrusting out her modest chest.

I see, he he! Then know this, human! You face the mighty Perpetua, and will rue the day you made the {i}dire{/i} mistake of opposing her! By the end of this bout, you will be but another one of my {i}many{/i} slaves, mwahahaha!
Accompanying her triumphant laugh, she sweepingly gestures around to accentuate her point and apparent power, flauntingly showing off... nothing that you can see.
Nevertheless, this inconsistency doesn't seem to deter her, the gooey girl carrying on unflappably with a superior snicker.

In fact, it would probably be for the best if you just gave up now and spared yourself the {i}withering humiliation{/i} of losing to the great Perpetua! Indeed, the moment you're in my pussy, I will have you {i}begging{/i} for more!
Ah, er, I mean- The moment your {i}dick{/i} is in my pussy... Not, like, your whole body... {size=-5}That would be weird...{/size}
While she self-consciously half-mumbles to herself, you decide to...
As you affirm your will to fight her, a smug smile returns to the red slime's face, the villainous goo girl standing to her full diminutive height and grandiosely trumpeting.
Ha! So be it, brave {i}hero{/i}! I will b-break you in an instant!
She seems rather nervous about this...
Ha! So be it, brave {i}hero{/i}! I will-
Wait, what? D-Did you just say you're giving up?!
Th-This has never happened before... Y-You can't do that!
Nobody will take me seriously if you just give up, you pervert! We have to {i}fight{/i}! A-At least try!? You're not going to just lie down and surrender to me, are you!?
Before you can answer, she angrily charges at you! It looks like she won't let you just give up... You could at least fake resistance, maybe?
You return to the clearing where you first encountered Perpetua.

As you approach the lair(?) of the villainous(?) slime girl, just like before, you hear her grandiloquently yell to herself as she rehearses various quips and taunts, though she promptly stops as you come into view.
Mwahaha! So you have returned, hero! Do you think you actually stand a chance against me? Let's see what you got, you pervert!
{size=-5}A-And give it a bit more spirit this time, okay?{/size}

And thus is the Town rid of its malevolent vandal!

You don't think you'll see her again.


...Probably.

For the time being, with your Quest complete and your first adventure wrapped up, you return to Town, finding the way back to be surprisingly quick compared to the trek you had to go through in order to get to Perpetua in the first place, the return trip actually taking somehow no more than a few moments... Probably something to do with your Grimoire's pathfinding magic?
Shelving that question for later, you enter the Town then go straight to the guild hall to claim the reward from Elena.

The guildswoman happening to be free, she receives you without delay, though she doesn't appear to be in the best of moods at the moment, seeming to be half-listening to you with a distant glum look in her tired, blank eyes. However, as soon as you bring up your success, she perks up instantly, her glassy gaze lighting up as she straightens up in her chair.
You dealt with the vandal? R-Really?!

Before you can even answer, a slightly teary-eyed Elena reaches over the guild counter and effortlessly pulls you into a downright bone-crushing hug, the lady turning out to have a {i}lot{/i} of strength in those dainty-looking arms of hers, the pressure of her embrace alongside the fact that she has stuffed your head into her cleavage making it hard to breathe.
Oh, thank you {i}so much{/i}! Nobody was taking that request seriously!

You respond with a powerless muffled noise as she happily shakes you around in her vice-like grip, her arms wrapped around your body leaving you unable to express any protest, whether by voice or by gesture... You also think you heard a bone crack.
Fortunately, just as you begin seeing stars, Elena suddenly breaks the hug, leaving you gasping for air while she straightens out her outfit with an embarrassed blush.
{i}Ahem...{/i} Well, {i}moving on{/i}! For your valiant efforts, here is your reward, with a bonus from me!

Swiftly, she opens one of her desk's drawers and fishes out a sizable-looking bag which she drops into your hands, the purse seeming to contain every Ero of your promised bounty and then some... At least, as far as you can judge in your current winded state.
And with that, you also qualify for the Forest Dungeon! Just let me...

As she speaks, Elena reaches out for your Grimoire, a small pulse of magic coursing from her to the book as she touches it, subtly altering its pathfinding capabilities. While you make a mental note to check your updated map whenever you'll next leave the Town, the guildswoman goes on to explain.
The first sigil is there, guarded by one of the Demon Queen's commanders... But after whatever terror you faced during this Quest, you're definitely ready!

As you stash your payment and quickly check yourself to make sure you're truly safe and whole, a magical ping rings behind Elena, seeming to originate from one of a series of boxes set along the wall, which she swiftly opens with a practiced gesture, fishing out a small folded piece of paper that she glances at before chuckling.
Well, isn't that convenient! You got a letter!
Turning back to you, she hands you the note, which seems a bit... damp?

With your business taken care of, you thank Elena who sees you off with renewed congratulations for your success which seems to have done wonders for her mood, and leave her desk to check the letter, somewhat struggling to unfold the soggy paper without damaging it, the source of the dampness turning out to be a rather distinctive and sticky reddish goo.
You're not sure what to think of that, but at any rate, Quest complete!
Go see Elena at the Guild counter.
